era_context: Early Bronze Age III. Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty 5–6), Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia, and the early Indus Valley Civilization are the primary literate civilizations. No major documented events are recorded for this year in any surviving source tradition.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The year -2860 is archaeologically and textually silent. The historical record for this period is dominated by Egyptian monumental inscriptions and Mesopotamian city-state records, both of which are selective and elite-focused. The vast majority of human activity—subsistence agriculture, local trade, family life, technological innovation at the village level—is invisible to our sources. The absence of documented events for -2860 reflects the limitations of the historical record, not the absence of human history.
